answers.field_label: Describe Any Change Improvement Or Decline
answers.value:
Week of service: 2026-01-11 through 2026-01-17.

Overall, Helen remained generally stable this week with a few minor but noteworthy observations:

1) Mobility and pain levels:
- Helen continues to ambulate short distances inside the apartment using her four-wheel walker with seat. She requires stand-by to light hands-on assistance for transfers from bed to chair and from chair to toilet.
- On Sunday (2026-01-11) she reported her usual morning stiffness in both knees due to osteoarthritis, rating her pain at about 4/10. After assisted stretching (gentle PROM to knees and hips) and walking a few laps around the living room with her walker, she reported improvement to about 2/10.
- On Tuesday (2026-01-13) afternoon, after we returned from a grocery trip and pharmacy stop, she reported a bit more fatigue and mild soreness in her lower back (about 5/10) from standing in line. She declined additional pain medication beyond her scheduled dose but did agree to rest in her recliner with a heating pad. After about 45 minutes of rest and repositioning, she said her pain was back down to 3/10.
- On Thursday (2026-01-15) morning she moved more slowly than usual getting out of bed and mentioned she had slept on her left side longer than normal. PROM and slow, supported walking again helped, and by the end of the shift she said she felt “looser than when we started.”
- On Saturday (2026-01-17) there was no significant change in gait or balance compared to earlier in the week. She still needs reminders to keep both hands on the walker and not to rush when turning.

2) Skin integrity and circulation:
- I checked her lower legs and heels each visit while assisting with dressing, hygiene, and transfers. There were no open areas, no redness on heels, and no new bruising noted.
- She has some long-standing mild swelling around both ankles that tends to increase later in the day. Elevation on pillows during rest and gentle ankle pumps were performed each shift. Swelling remained mild and symmetric with no warmth or discoloration.
- No pressure areas observed on sacrum or hips when assisting with repositioning. I reminded Helen to shift her weight and change positions at least every 1–2 hours while sitting in her recliner.

3) Respiratory status and oxygen use:
- Helen uses home oxygen via nasal cannula at 2 L/min as needed, mostly in the mornings and when she feels short of breath. Throughout this week, her breathing was generally stable.
- On Sunday and Thursday mornings she used her oxygen for roughly 1–1.5 hours during our visit. Respirations were even and unlabored, and she was able to speak in full sentences.
- No coughing fits, wheezing, or complaints of chest pain were reported. She did note feeling “a little winded” after walking back from the bedroom to the kitchen on Tuesday, which resolved with a brief seated rest and deep-breathing exercises.

4) Mood, cognition, and behavior:
- Helen was oriented to person, place, and time at all visits. She occasionally repeated a question about what day her next doctor’s appointment is (upcoming later in the month), but with a written note on the fridge she seemed reassured.
- Her mood was generally pleasant and cooperative. On Tuesday she seemed a bit more anxious about going out in icy weather, but we took extra time, used proper footwear, and she expressed relief once we were safely back home.
- She engaged in conversation about her grandchildren and upcoming plans to have a video call with her daughter, which appeared to brighten her mood.

5) Nutrition, hydration, and elimination:
- Appetite remained fair to good. She ate light breakfasts (oatmeal or toast with peanut butter and fruit) on the morning shifts and a full, balanced dinner on Tuesday (baked chicken, brown rice, steamed vegetables) that we prepared together.
- She is drinking water and herbal tea but still needs frequent reminders to sip fluids throughout the day. I encouraged her to keep a water bottle within reach of her recliner.
- Bowel movements: she reported regular bowel movements every 1–2 days with no constipation or diarrhea during this week. Urination is regular with no burning or urgency reported.

6) Safety and environment:
- No falls or near-falls reported this week. She continues to respond well to reminders to use her walker and not to carry items in her hands while walking.
- I checked that pathways in the living room and hallway stayed clear of clutter. We moved a small rug that had started to curl at the edge to reduce tripping risk.
- Smoke detector in the hallway was tested on Thursday at her request and beeped appropriately.

Summary:
Helen’s condition this week can be described as stable with mild, expected fluctuations in joint pain and fatigue related to activity, but no major decline or acute changes in health, safety, or mental status. Pain and stiffness improved with gentle exercise, repositioning, and rest. No new skin issues, respiratory problems, or elimination concerns were noted. I will continue to monitor her mobility, pain levels, and oxygen use closely and will report any significant changes to the supervisor and her family as needed.
